description: Template defining the structured report each engage-prism subagent must return. Includes a citations[] array (every entry conforms to citation-schema.json) and a webResearch object documenting whether live web research was performed.

reportFields.label: Angle
reportFields.description: The name of the analytical angle being explored.

reportFields.label: Core Claim
reportFields.description: 1-3 sentences. The headline answer this angle produces. Must be supported by at least one cited source (or explicitly flagged as derived/speculative).

reportFields.label: Web Research
reportFields.description: Documents whether live web research was performed.

subfields.label: Reason
subfields.description: If performed=false, why not (sandbox, --no-web, unavailable tool).

subfields.label: Fetch count
subfields.description: Number of WebFetch/WebSearch calls made. Must be 0 if performed=false.

subfields.label: Attempted calls
subfields.description: Evidence that at least one fetch was attempted. REQUIRED (minItems: 1) when performed=false — bare claims of tool unavailability without an attempted-call record are rejected and re-dispatched. Each entry: { method: 'WebFetch'|'WebSearch', targetUrl?, query?, errorMessage, httpStatus?, attemptedAt (ISO-8601) }. May be empty when performed=true (fetchCount alone documents success).

reportFields.label: Citations
reportFields.description: Every citation MUST conform to citation-schema.json. May be empty only when webResearch.performed=false.

reportFields.label: Evidence Base
reportFields.description: Summary of the evidence underlying this angle.

subfields.label: Source mix
subfields.description: E.g., '3 analyst reports, 1 regulator filing, 2 company pages'.

subfields.label: Recency profile
subfields.description: E.g., 'all within last 90 days' or 'oldest: Q3 2024, newest: today'.

subfields.label: Known gaps
subfields.description: At least one gap — what evidence you would have wanted but could not obtain.

reportFields.label: Analysis
reportFields.description: Ordered analytical steps. Each step names the claim, the citations it rests on (by index into citations[]), and any derived inference.

reportFields.label: Key Numbers / Drivers
reportFields.description: Quantitative anchors (e.g., 'TAM: $4.2B', 'CAC: $180', 'Revenue growth: 11% YoY'). Each must name the source (cited) or be labeled 'estimated' with the basis.

reportFields.label: Counter-Evidence and Risks
reportFields.description: Things that would falsify or weaken the core claim, preferably with citations.

reportFields.label: Recommendation (within this angle)
reportFields.description: 1-3 sentences. The best-supported answer from this angle alone, honest about limits.

subfields.label: Reason
subfields.description: 1 sentence — how well does this angle fit the specific question?

synthesisNotes: Every non-derived claim must reference a citation. Claims without citations are treated as speculation in synthesis.
Fit score is a signal, not ground truth — subagents briefed to explore one angle will naturally find reasons it works.
If webResearch.performed=false, treat the entire report as degraded evidence and flag that explicitly in the final recommendation.

If webResearch.performed=false AND webResearch.attemptedCalls is empty, REJECT the report and re-dispatch the subagent once with an explicit 'attempt at least one fetch before declaring unavailability' directive. If the retry also returns zero attempts, accept the degraded report but tag degradationEvidence='unverified' so synthesis deprioritizes it further.

Recency gate: for fast-moving topics (freshnessClass geopolitical/market threshold 24h; general 72h), if the freshest cited anchor source exceeds the threshold, REJECT the path and re-dispatch once with 'fetch a source dated within the last {threshold}h' directive. If retry cannot satisfy the gate, emit '⚠️ Recency-gate degraded: freshest citation Xh old (threshold Yh)' in the path Report — never silent.

Anchor corroboration: a probability weight or price level must be supported by ≥2 independent citations (distinct domains) before it drives synthesis. Single-source anchors are tagged anchorEvidence='single-source' and deprioritized.
